look.text: My new desk. It's got a lamp, a sticky note, and the faint outline of where a monitor used to be. They really rolled out the red carpet.
look.speaker: casey

look__sticky_note.text: 'Welcome! —JR.' Jordan Reeves, my manager who's on PTO. Three letters on a sticky note. That's my entire onboarding.
look__sticky_note.speaker: casey

use.text: I'd love to start working, but I literally have no computer. Can't exactly modernize government systems with a desk lamp and good vibes.
use.speaker: casey

use_laptop.text: I place the laptop on the desk, open it, and sit down. The desk lamp flickers on with a tap. Time to work.
use_laptop.speaker: casey

look.text: An HP LaserJet with 47 jobs in the queue and a blinking paper jam light. Somewhere in government, there is a printer that works. This is not that printer.
look.speaker: casey

use.text: I open the panel. Paper jam. Classic. I could probably fix this but I've got bigger fish to fry.
use.speaker: casey

use_cup_of_water.text: I pour a little water on the stuck paper to loosen it... and it actually works. The jam clears. 47 jobs start printing immediately. The entire bullpen turns to look at me.
use_cup_of_water.speaker: casey

look.text: The coffee maker is empty. The 'brew' light is on. In government, this is classified as a Level 1 incident.
look.speaker: casey

use.text: No coffee grounds, no filters. Someone took the last cup and didn't make more. In any other context this would be a misdemeanor. Here it's just Tuesday.
use.speaker: casey

look.text: 'CLEAN YOUR OWN DISHES — MGMT.' This sign has been here so long it's become load-bearing. Remove it and the entire social contract of this office collapses.
look.speaker: casey

use.text: I open the door. Something in here smells like a war crime. I close the door.
use.speaker: casey

look.text: Every container is labeled. 'DAVE'S — DO NOT TOUCH.' 'PROPERTY OF ACCOUNTING.' 'THIS IS NOT YOUR YOGURT.' The territorial politics of a shared fridge could fill a doctoral thesis.
look.speaker: casey

use.text: I find one unlabeled soda in the back. It's been here since the Obama administration. I decide to leave it. Some things are best left undisturbed.
use.speaker: casey

look.text: This desk belongs to someone who cares. Sticky notes in color-coded clusters, user journey maps that actually make sense, a plant that someone waters. This is my kind of person.
look.speaker: casey

look__whiteboard.text: This is a complete map of the current benefits application process. 47 steps. Three different systems. Two manual handoffs. And a fax machine. It's a horror movie in flowchart form. But it also means someone already understands the problem.
look__whiteboard.speaker: casey

look__whiteboard_deep.text: Wait. Steps 12 through 17 are the applicant re-submitting documents that were already captured in Step 3. The system doesn't carry data forward between stages. That's six redundant steps.
look__whiteboard_deep.speaker: casey

look.text: Processing time: 47 days. Backlog: twelve thousand applications. Satisfaction: 23 percent. Every number on this dashboard is a person waiting for help they were promised. I'm going to change these numbers.
look.speaker: casey

use.text: I can't interact with it from here. But I'm memorizing these numbers. They're my scoreboard for the next 90 days.
use.speaker: casey

look.text: The War Room. Through the glass I can see a sprint board that hasn't been updated in weeks. Some cards are in 'In Progress.' None are in 'Done.' That tracks.
look.speaker: casey

use.text: Locked. I need the right badge access level. Or, more likely, I need to find whoever has the key.
use.speaker: casey

look.text: Day 1 of 90. Ninety days to modernize an agency that's been running on duct tape and COBOL since before I was born. No pressure.
look.speaker: casey

use.text: I straighten the banner. If we're going to save this place, we should at least have straight signage.
use.speaker: casey

look.text: One jacket, unclaimed. It's been here so long it's practically a historical artifact. Someone left D.A.S.H. in such a hurry they forgot their coat. That's either very good or very bad.
look.speaker: casey

look__jacket_pocket.text: There's a crumpled sticky note in the pocket. The ink is faded and smudged. I can make out: 'Srv rm pw: adm_n1___'. Some kind of password, but half the characters are gone. I'll hang onto this.
look__jacket_pocket.speaker: casey

pickup.text: I take the umbrella. You never know.
pickup.speaker: casey

look.text: The supply closet. Locked with a keypad. In a government office, the supply closet is more secure than most data centers.
look.speaker: casey

use.text: I try the handle. Locked. The keypad has four digits. I try 1234. Nothing. I try 0000. Nothing. I need the actual code.
use.speaker: casey

use_supply_closet_code.text: Priya's code works. Inside: monitors, keyboards, mice, cables, and... a stack of brand new laptops still in their boxes. A supply closet full of equipment that nobody can access because the person who set the code retired. Government.
use_supply_closet_code.speaker: casey

look.text: His monitor shows 4,000 unread emails. His face shows zero emotion. This man has transcended.
look.speaker: casey

look.text: Priya Kapoor. UX Researcher. The pen behind her ear looks like it has been there so long it is load-bearing.
look.speaker: casey

look: Not what I'm looking for right now.
I've seen enough beige for one lifetime.
use: That doesn't help me get a computer.
I should focus on finding a laptop first.
pickup: That's bolted down. Like everything else in a government office.
Pretty sure there's a procurement form for that.
talkto: They don't notice me. Story of my first day.
They're in the zone. I respect it.

hintMessages.ENTER: I should check out my desk and get the lay of the land.
hintMessages.NO_COMPUTER: I need a computer. Maybe someone here can help me find one.
hintMessages.MEET_PRIYA: Priya wants me to prove I'm serious before she gives me the supply closet code. I need to show her I'm not just another talker.
hintMessages.PROVE_WHITEBOARD: I should take another close look at Priya's whiteboard. There might be something useful in that process map.
hintMessages.PROVE_PRINTER: That printer is still jammed. If only I had some water to loosen the stuck paper...
hintMessages.CODE_GET: Priya gave me the code. Time to open that supply closet.
hintMessages.LAPTOP_GET: I've got a laptop. Now I just need to set it up at my desk.
